BulletinMeasurements.highlights: Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches easily.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In little used terrain a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northwesterly wind, further wind slabs will form in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain as well as above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Winter sport participants can release avalanches easily. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers and reach large size. This applies on west, north and east facing slopes. The avalanche prone locations are numerous, especially in areas close to the tree line, as well as above the tree line. Natural avalanches are still even now possible.

In addition some wet and gliding avalanches are possible. This applies especially on cut and grassy slopes below approximately 1800 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Tuesday. The wind will be strong to storm force in some cases. The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The rain will give rise as the day progresses to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack at low altitude.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Hardly any decrease in avalanche danger. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Wind slabs remain prone to triggering. Caution is to be exercised in particular on west, north and east facing slopes in areas close to the tree line, as well as above the tree line. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found also on southeast, south and southwest facing slopes above approximately 2400 m. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers of the snowpack.
Additionally avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation wet loose snow avalanches are possible. This applies especially on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
5 to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the favourable surface of an old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies especially on very steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Hardly any decrease in avalanche danger. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular in extremely steep terrain. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The weather will be mild. In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: At low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.

In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very mild. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Ground avalanches are possible in particular in the afternoon.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular in extremely steep terrain. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The weather will be mild. In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: At low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers.

The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.

In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very mild. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Ground avalanches are possible in particular in the afternoon.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh and older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In particular here the avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

Night: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m. With the freezing level at 3000 metres, combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days.
Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and caution. Careful route selection is recommended. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
A lot of snow fell in the last ten days. As a consequence of snowfall the strong to storm force northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially along the border with France.
On Monday the wind was strong in some localities over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
In these regions at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some small and medium-sized moist avalanches have been released in the last few days. Isolated medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches have been released by people in the last few days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of natural wet avalanches will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh and older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. In particular here the avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach quite a large size.

Night: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 3000 m. With the freezing level at 3000 metres, combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days.
Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and caution. Careful route selection is recommended. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
A lot of snow fell in the last ten days. As a consequence of snowfall the strong to storm force northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially along the border with France.
On Monday the wind was strong in some localities over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
In these regions at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some small and medium-sized moist avalanches have been released in the last few days. Isolated medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches have been released by people in the last few days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of natural wet avalanches will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Significant increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. Ground avalanches at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The weather will be very warm. On very steep sunny slopes and at low altitude medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the strong northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially on near-ridge east, south and southwest facing slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.
Isolated medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last two days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very warm. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Ground avalanches are possible in particular in the afternoon.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Significant increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. Ground avalanches at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The weather will be very warm. On very steep sunny slopes and at low altitude medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

The fresh and older wind slabs can still be released. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released in isolated cases by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the strong northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially on near-ridge east, south and southwest facing slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In these regions above approximately 1300 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.
Isolated medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last two days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very warm. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Ground avalanches are possible in particular in the afternoon.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released easily. Wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. Wet avalanches as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls large surface-area wind slabs formed. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
On steep shady slopes the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches. Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The wind will be moderate to strong in some localities. In particular above approximately 1900 m clearly visible wind slabs formed.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep north, east and west facing slopes.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2300 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The weather will be warm. The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. In particular below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

Gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible on steep grassy slopes.

The weather conditions fostered a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released in some cases by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially on near-ridge northeast, south and southwest facing slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.
During the day: The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to significant moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very warm. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Moist avalanches can in some cases release the wet snowpack and reach large size in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The weather will be warm. The danger of wet and gliding avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
On sunny slopes and at low and intermediate altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. In particular below approximately 2600 m moist avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size.

Gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible on steep grassy slopes.

The weather conditions fostered a gradual stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but can be released in some cases by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days, especially on near-ridge northeast, south and southwest facing slopes, as well as at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.
During the day: The spring-like weather conditions from the early morning will give rise to significant moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m. The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very warm. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Moist avalanches can in some cases release the wet snowpack and reach large size in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow. Above the tree line avalanche prone wind slabs formed, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
On very steep sunny slopes medium-sized natural avalanches must be expected. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Mostly the dry avalanches are medium-sized and can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The snowpack remains prone to triggering over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist. The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Shady slopes: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above the tree line. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m. The runout zones are to be treated with caution.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The activity of wet avalanches will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of the strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. They can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. The avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Night: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of avalanches being released will increase.
With the freezing level at 3000 metres, combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days.
Careful route selection is advisable. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow fell in the last ten days.
In the last few days the wind was moderate to strong. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Some medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last few days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In particular steep sunny slopes base of rock walls: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of the strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. They can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. The avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Night: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of avalanches being released will increase.
With the freezing level at 3000 metres, combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, some wet and moist avalanches are still possible at medium mountain altitudes on slopes that have not yet been completely cleared in recent days.
Careful route selection is advisable. Backcountry tours should be concluded early.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Some snow fell in the last ten days.
In the last few days the wind was moderate to strong. Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Some medium-sized slab avalanches have been released by people in the last few days.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be warm. The weather conditions will foster a stabilisation of the snow drift accumulations. The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: The avalanche danger is within the uppermost range of danger level 3 (considerable).
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Shady slopes: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Very steep sunny slopes: Increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above the tree line. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

Wind slabs remain prone to triggering. Caution is to be exercised in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m. The runout zones are to be treated with caution as well.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the snow. Further wind slabs will form.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Hardly any decrease in avalanche danger. The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will still be encountered.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The wind slabs of the last few days represent the main danger. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on sunny slopes.
Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on steep sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be mostly sunny. Further warming to the high Alpine regions.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Gliding avalanches and moist snow slides require caution. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.

The older wind slabs can still be released at intermediate and high altitudes. These can in some places be released by people and reach medium size.
On steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.
Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very mild. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Moist avalanches can in some cases release the wet snowpack and reach large size in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Gliding avalanches and moist snow slides require caution. As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. Backcountry tours should be started very early and concluded timely.

The older wind slabs can still be released at intermediate and high altitudes. These can in some places be released by people and reach medium size.
On steep grassy slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible. Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
By Saturday sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise from early morning to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.
Since Friday the weather has been very mild. In particular below the tree line: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be very mild. The high humditiy will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as the moisture increases. Moist avalanches can in some cases release the wet snowpack and reach large size in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant. In their paths avalanches can entrain the wet snow. This extends the avalanche runout distances.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The activity of wet avalanches will gradually increase.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet avalanches are possible. This applies in particular on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m.
Restraint is advisable.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported some snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are the main danger. Slab avalanches are possible in isolated cases as before.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Caution is to be exercised in areas with glide cracks. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of gliding avalanches being released will increase quickly in particular on steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes. Gliding avalanches are possible at any time, even large ones in isolated cases. They can also be released in the morning. In addition on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes, further medium-sized slab avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
The wind slabs can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
And in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually on steep south facing slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack will consolidate.
